content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: LAX Lost & Found
- url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found
\
  - source: Los Angeles International Airport
- domain: flylax.com

## Content
\
  
# LAX Lost & Found

## Who We Are

The LAX Lost and Found is managed by the\
  \ Airport Police Division. The mission of the Airport Police Lost and Found Unit\
  \ is to reunite the public with lost items left in public areas such as baggage\
  \ claim, gate areas, and airport parking lots.

The Lost and Found Unit does not\
  \ manage or collect lost items left in TSA areas or on aircraft. A claim must be\
  \ submitted to inquire about a lost item. There is no cost to the public to submit\
  \ an inquiry. A lost-and-found claim is a method of inquiry and is not a police\
  \ report.

LAX Airport Police Lost and Found is not affiliated with third-party\
  \ websites that offer to help search for lost items or charge a service fee.

\
  ## Areas Managed by LAX Airport Police Lost and Found

Use the LAX online lost-item\
  \ inquiry for property left in public airport areas including:

- Ticketing areas
\
  - Baggage claim
- Gate areas
- Restaurants
- Stores
- Curbside areas
- Airport\
  \ parking lots
- The LAX-IT shuttle and LAX-IT lot

## Other Lost and Found Inquiries
\
  
Different locations and services use different lost-property channels:

- For\
  \ items left in TSA areas, use TSA Lost and Found.
- For a lost suitcase or an\
  \ item left on an aircraft, contact the airline.
- For items left with dining or\
  \ shopping businesses, use the relevant dining or shopping contact.
- For items\
  \ left with transportation services, use the relevant transportation provider.
\
  - LAX employee shuttle buses are operated by ABM.
- FlyAway lost-and-found concerns\
  \ use 1-866-iFlyLAX.
- For lost passports involving U.S. Customs, call 310-665-4560.
\
  
## Frequently Asked Questions

### How do I inquire about an item lost in a\
  \ public area?

The inquiry method is online. Submit a claim on the LAX Lost and\
  \ Found website for an item lost in public areas such as ticketing, curbside, baggage\
  \ claim, gate areas, stores, LAX-IT, or airport parking lots.

### Is there a\
  \ fee to submit a claim?

No. LAX Airport Police does not charge a fee to inquire\
  \ about a lost item.

### Why does the form request identifying information?
\
  
The information helps the team identify the owner and return the item to the correct\
  \ person. Owner verification is required.

### How will the team communicate?
\
  
The team primarily communicates by email and may call for additional information.\
  \ Messages may come from the software provider domains used by the Lost and Found\
  \ office.

### Can I call to inquire about an item?

The inquiry process is\
  \ online. There is no telephone inquiry process for the LAX Airport Police Lost\
  \ and Found claim workflow.

### How long does review take?

Claims are reviewed\
  \ Monday through Friday, excluding holidays. Average response time is about five\
  \ to seven business days, although timing varies.

### How is an item returned?
\
  
If an item is matched, the traveler may receive shipping instructions and information\
  \ about an in-person return. Shipping is at the owner’s expense.
"

title: LAX Lost & Found
url: https://www.flylax.com/lax-comments-and-contact-us/lost-and-found

args.question: Where do you think you last had the tablet: in a TSA screening area, on the aircraft, at the gate/terminal or baggage claim, in a shop/restaurant, on a shuttle/transport service, or in a parking/curbside area? If you are unsure, tell me the last place you remember using it and your airline.
args.reason: LAX routes lost items to different organizations depending on the exact location, so this detail is needed to identify the correct report channel.
